1.description: Currency fixes across time zones. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

2.description: Cumulative five-minute returns for EUR, GBP, and JPY. This figure displays cumulative average five-minute returns (Δs$\Delta s$) over the course of a trading day for the EUR (dashed line), GBP (dash-dotted line), and JPY (dotted line). An increase means the foreign currency appreciates against the U.S. dollar. The three black dashed lines at 20:55, 8:15, and 11:00 refer to the Tokyo, ECB, and London fixes, respectively. Returns are expressed in bps. Time is measured in ET. The sample period is January 1999 to December 2019. The data are from Refinitiv Tick History. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

3.description: Intraday return dynamics: Dollar portfolio. This figure displays cumulative average five-minute returns (Δs$\Delta s$) for the dollar portfolio over the course of a trading day. An increase means the basket of foreign currencies appreciates against the U.S. dollar. The three black dashed lines at 20:55, 8:15, and 11:00 refer to the Tokyo, ECB, and London fixes, respectively. Returns are expressed in bps. Time is measured in ET. The sample period is January 1999 to December 2019. The data are from Refinitiv Tick History.

4.description: Total return indices and year-over-year performance: Trading theVs. The panels show the performance of a trading strategy around the Tokyo and Europe fixes, where an investor takes a long position in the U.S. dollar during the prefix and a short position during the postfix. The top panels show the total return indices for the three major currencies (EUR, GBP, and JPY) and the dollar portfolio (DOL) with an initial investment of one U.S. dollar. The bottom panels show the total returns by year for the dollar portfolio. See Note 2 in theAppendixfor the definition of reversal strategies. The sample period is January 1999 to December 2019. The data are from Refinitiv Tick History. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

5.description: Reversal returns: Alternative reversal times. The figure shows reversal returns with different reversal times during the main Tokyo (Panel A) and European (Panel B) trading hours. Returns are measured in bps (y-axis). Thex-axis shows the timing of the reversal point relative to the Tokyo and European fixes (indicated by the value “0” and marked by the black bar). Values to the left (right) of the black bars indicate that the reversal occurs earlier (later) than the fix. See Note 2 in theAppendixfor the definition of reversal strategies. The sample period is January 1999 to December 2019. The data are from Refinitiv Tick History.

6.description: Intraday returns dynamics: Daylight savings time changes. This figure displays cumulative average five-minute returns (Δs$\Delta s$), expressed in bps, for the EUR (Panel A), GBP (Panel B), and JPY (Panel C) during the main trading hours in Japan on days when the time zone in New York is Eastern Daylight Saving Time (DST, dotted line) and Eastern Standard Time (No DST, solid line). An increase means foreign currencies appreciate against the U.S. dollar. The two dashed lines at 19:55 and 20:55 refer to the time of the Tokyo fix in each time zone. The sample period is January 1999 to December 2019. The data are from Refinitiv Tick History. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

7.description: Intraday returns dynamics: Introduction of the London fix. This figure displays cumulative average five-minute returns (Δs$\Delta s$), expressed in bps, for the EUR (Panel A), GBP (Panel B), and JPY (Panel C) during the main trading hours in London and New York. The dotted red line refers to the period February 1986 to December 1993 before the London fix was introduced. The solid blue line refers to the period January 1999 to December 2019. The two dashed vertical lines refer to the ECB fix (8:15 ET) and the London fix (11:00 ET), respectively. The data are from Olsen Financial Technologies for the years prior to 1999, and from Refinitiv Tick History for 1999 onward. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

8.description: Changes in quoting activity around the London fix. The figure shows the number of quote updates in each five-minute interval in currency futures traded on the CME relative to the daily total number of submitted quotes between 8:30 ET and 14:30 ET for the GBP and the JPY. Panels A and B (top row) contrast futures market activity in the period July 1980 to December 1993 (dotted line) with the period January 1999 to December 2017 (solid line). Panels C and D (bottom row) show the quoting activity 30-minutes before and after the London fix during the period July 1980 to December 1993 (dotted line), between January 1995 to December 1998 (dashed line), between January 1999 to May 2006 (dash-dotted line), and between June 2006 to December 2017 (solid line). The data are from CME. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

9.description: Relative trading volume around the clock. The figure displays the average hourly trading volume over the course of the trading day relative to daily total trading volume expressed in percent. The values in each column add up to 100. The sample period is June 2006 to December 2019. The data are from Refinitiv FX Matching. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

10.description: Normalized order flow dynamics. The panels show the average normalized order flow dynamics in every hourly interval over the course of the main trading hours in Europe and the United States for the AUD (Panel A) and the GBP (Panel B). Normalized order flow is defined as buyer- minus seller-initiated traded volume relative to total trading volume. The sample period is June 2006 to December 2019. The data are from Refinitiv FX Matching.
